A woman who does not respect God, who is willful and wasteful, and enjoys comfort, will just as surely ruin her family as if she tore her house down.
Here is grace and sin in their true colors. Those who despise God’s precepts and promises despise God and all of His power and mercy.
Pride grows from that root of bitterness which is in the heart. The root must be removed, or we cannot overcome this branch. Wise men’s thoughtful words help them avoid difficulties.
There can be no benefit unless there is something, even if it seems small, that will discourage those who are lazy.
A careful witness will not risk representing anything other than what he knows.
A scoffer treats divine things with contempt. Whoever recognizes their own ignorance and unworthiness will search the Scriptures with a humble spirit.
We discover a wicked person if there is no evidence of reverence in their speech.

We are travelers, whose concern is not to look for wonders, but to reach our journey’s end; to understand the rules we are to follow, and also the goals we are to pursue. A wicked person deceives himself and continues in his error.
Foolish and unholy people think sin is a small thing, to be dismissed instead of grieved over. Fools ridicule the sacrifice for sin; but those who dismiss sin also dismiss Christ.
We do not know what feelings of guilt or overwhelming desires trouble the wealthy sinner. Nor does the world know the peace of mind a sincere Christian experiences, even in poverty and illness.
Sin damages many prominent families, while righteousness frequently elevates and strengthens even humble families.
The ways of carelessness, of worldliness, and of sensuality seem right to those who walk in them, but self-deceivers prove to be self-destroyers. See the emptiness of worldly pleasure.
Of all sinners, those who fall away will experience the greatest fear when they consider their actions.

A quick willingness to believe what others say has always been harmful. The entire world was ruined in this way initially. The person who is spiritually wise relies only on the Savior for acceptance. They are watchful against the enemies of their salvation by paying attention to God’s word.
Reverence protects against everything unholy.
An angry person is to be pitied as well as blamed, but a vengeful person is even more hateful.
Sin is the shame of sinners; but wisdom is the honor of the wise.
Even wicked people recognize the excellence of God’s people.
Friendship in the world is controlled by self-interest. It is good to have God as your friend; he will not abandon you.
To look down on someone because of their job or how they appear is a sin.
How wisely people consider their own advantage who not only do good, but plan for it!
Work done with your mind or hands will be worthwhile. But if people’s faith is only expressed through talking and commotion, they will achieve nothing.
The wealth of wise and devout people increases their ability to be helpful.
A righteous person will risk the disapproval of even the most powerful people to reveal the truth.
People who fear the Lord so that they obey and serve him have good reason for confidence and will be protected. Let us seek this fountain of life so that we may avoid the traps of death.
Let everyone who wishes well for the Kingdom of Christ do what they can so that many may be added to his church.
A gentle, patient person learns from Christ, who is wisdom itself. Uncontrolled passion reveals foolishness.
A virtuous, content, and kind mind tends to health.
Oppressing the poor reproaches our Creator.

The wicked person has their soul taken from them; they die in their sins, under the guilt and power of those sins. But faithful people, even though they experience pain and some fear of death, have the blessed hope that God, who cannot lie, has given them.
Wisdom influences the heart, and therefore governs feelings and moods.
Piety and holiness always promote hard work, moderation, and honesty.

The great King who reigns over heaven and earth will reward faithful servants who honor his gospel by properly fulfilling the responsibilities of their positions; he does not despise the service of even the least.
